requirements.full_sentence:
All Supervised Entities (SEs) are required to submit certain
supervisory returns to the Reserve Bank as per various directions / circulars/
notifications issued by the Bank from time to time.
requirements.object: returns to the Reserve Bank as per various directions / circulars/ notifications issued by the Bank from time to time

requirements.full_sentence:
These Directions shall be called the Master Direction - Reserve Bank of India (Filing
of Supervisory Returns) Directions - 2024.
requirements.object: Direction - Reserve Bank of India (Filing of Supervisory Returns) Directions - 2024

requirements.full_sentence: These Directions shall come into immediate effect.
These Directions shall apply to the following entities:
requirements.object: entities:

requirements.full_sentence:
above shall hereafter be referred to as
'Supervised Entities (SEs)' for the purpose of these Directions.
requirements.object: as 'Supervised Entities (SEs)' for the purpose of these Directions

requirements.full_sentence:
Commercial Banks and NBFCs shall be required to report data on their domestic
and overseas operations, including the operations of IFSC Banking Units (IBUs) and
Overseas Banking Units (OBUs), wherever applicable.
requirements.object: data on their domestic and overseas operations, including the operations of IFSC Banking Units (IBUs) and Overseas Banking Units (OBUs)

requirements.full_sentence:
These Directions shall be in addition to, and not in derogation of any other
Instructions / Directions issued by the RBI, except for such clauses in the instructions
/ Directions that have been repealed herein.
requirements.object: and not in derogation of any other Instructions / Directions issued by the RBI

requirements.full_sentence:
In these Directions, unless the context otherwise requires, the terms herein shall
bear the meanings assigned to them as below:
a.
requirements.object: to them as below: a

requirements.full_sentence:
The 'Principal Business' criteria may be referred to as
given in RBI's Press Release No.
requirements.object: given in RBI's Press Release No

requirements.full_sentence:
Base, Middle, Upper and Top Layers NBFCs shall have the same meaning
as given in RBI's notification DoR.FIN.REC.No.45/03.10.119/2023-24 dated
October 19, 2023 (Master Direction - Reserve Bank of India (Non-Banking
Financial Company - Scale Based Regulation) Directions, 2023) and
subsequent clarifications thereon.
requirements.object: as given in RBI's notification DoR

requirements.full_sentence:
All other expressions unless defined herein shall have the same meaning as have
been assigned to them under the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934, or Banking
Regulation Act, 1949, or Securitisation and Reconstruction of Financial Assets and
Enforcement of Security Interest Act, 2002 or Glossary of Terms published by Reserve
Bank or as used in commercial parlance, as the case may be.
requirements.object: as have been assigned to them under the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934, or Banking Regulation Act, 1949, or Securitisation and

requirements.full_sentence:
The risk data aggregation capabilities and risk reporting practices should be
fully documented and subject to high standards of validation that are aligned
with bank's other independent risk management reviews.
requirements.object: subject to high standards of validation that are aligned with bank's other independent risk management reviews

requirements.full_sentence:
The validation of
risk data aggregation and risk reporting practices shall be conducted using
staff with specific IT, data and reporting expertise.
requirements.object: with specific IT, data and reporting expertise

requirements.full_sentence:
Board and senior
management should ensure that adequate resources are deployed for the
purpose.
requirements.object: are deployed for the purpose

requirements.full_sentence:
The Board and senior management shall include the identification,
assessment, and management of data quality risks as part of its overall risk
management framework.
requirements.object: and management of data quality risks as part of its overall risk management framework

requirements.full_sentence:
The framework should include standards for both
outsourced and in-house risk data-related processes, policies on data
confidentiality, integrity and availability, as well as risk management policies.
requirements.object: outsourced and in-house risk data-related processes, policies on data confidentiality, integrity and availability, as well

requirements.full_sentence:
While considering any acquisition / divestiture, new product development,
IT Change initiatives etc, the due diligence process should consider the
impact of such activities on the data aggregation and reporting.
requirements.object: such activities on the data aggregation and reporting

requirements.full_sentence:
In such
cases, it should be ensured that data aggregation and reporting facilities are
integrated within the existing reporting framework within a timeframe.
requirements.object: aggregation and reporting facilities are integrated within the existing reporting framework within a timeframe

requirements.full_sentence:
The Board and senior management shall ensure that ability of the SE to
aggregate and report data at a consolidated level or at any relevant level
within the organisation is not hindered by its group structure (eg sub-
consolidated level, jurisdiction of operation level).
requirements.object: the SE to aggregate and report data at a consolidated level or at any relevant level within the organisation is not hindered by

requirements.full_sentence:
In particular data
aggregation and reporting should be independent from the choices a SE
makes regarding its legal organisation and geographical presence, subject
to the statutory limitations, if any.
requirements.object: choices a SE makes regarding its legal organisation and geographical presence

requirements.full_sentence:
A SE shall design, build and maintain the data architecture and supporting
IT infrastructure for accurate, complete and timely data aggregation and
reporting not only in normal times but also during times of stress or crisis.
requirements.object: the data architecture and supporting IT infrastructure for accurate, complete and timely data aggregation and reporting not

requirements.full_sentence:
The data aggregation and reporting practices should be considered an
essential part of the SE's business continuity planning process and subject
to a business impact analysis.
requirements.object: part of the SE's business continuity planning process and subject to a business impact analysis

requirements.full_sentence:
Roles and responsibilities should be established among business owners
and IT team so as to ensure that the data is kept current and aligned with
the data definitions and with the SEs data reporting policies.
requirements.object: owners and IT team so as to ensure that the data is kept current and aligned with the data definitions and with the SEs

requirements.full_sentence:
SEs should ensure that resources and IT infrastructure is adequate to meet
a broad range of on-demand, ad hoc reporting requests, including requests
during stress / crises situation and to meet supervisory queries.
requirements.object: IT infrastructure is adequate to meet a broad range of on-demand, ad hoc reporting requests, including requests during stres

requirements.full_sentence:
For example, the SE should be able to accurately aggregate data
on exposure for a particular period for a specific industry cluster in a district.
requirements.object: aggregate data on exposure for a particular period for a specific industry cluster in a district

requirements.full_sentence:
All returns / risk reports should be reconciled with SEs own sources,
including accounting data where appropriate, to ensure accuracy and
completeness of the same.
requirements.object: own sources, including accounting data where appropriate, to ensure accuracy and completeness of the same

requirements.full_sentence:
SEs should strive to achieve a higher degree of automation in generation of
data for filing of returns.
requirements.object: higher degree of automation in generation of data for filing of returns

requirements.full_sentence:
SEs shall maintain proper records of sources and aggregation rules for
generating returns' data.
requirements.object: sources and aggregation rules for generating returns' data

requirements.full_sentence:
Timelines for filing of Returns: SEs shall submit the applicable returns as given
in Annex III, with accurate and complete data, strictly within the prescribed timelines.
requirements.object: as given in Annex III, with accurate and complete data, strictly within the prescribed timelines

requirements.full_sentence:
The principles for the same is tabulated below:
Periodicity Reference Date Timeline for Return Submission
Weekly Friday of the week On or before Wednesday of the following week
15th and Last day (28th/ 29th/ 30th/ 31st) of the respective month
Within 7 days from the Reference Date
Fortnightly
Last day (28th/ 29th/ 30th/ 31st) of a respective month
Within 15 days from the Reference Date
Monthly
Last day of the Calendar Quarter (i.e., March 31st, June 30th, September 30th, and December 31st)
Within 21 days from the Reference Date
Quarterly
Half yearly March 31st, and September 30th
Within 21 days from the Reference Date
Yearly March 31st Within 21 days from the Reference Date Note:
1) All audited returns, wherever applicable, shall be filed within 5 working days from the date of signing of the Auditor's report in terms of section 134 of the Companies Act, 2013 (solo/ group level as per applicability of the return), as applicable.
requirements.object: working days from the date of signing of the Auditor's report in terms of section 134 of the Companies Act, 2013 (solo/ group level

requirements.full_sentence: 2) All ad-hoc returns/ data must be submitted within the timelines as indicated in the communication issued by the RBI.
requirements.object: timelines as indicated in the communication issued by the RBI

requirements.full_sentence:
Ad-Hoc / Additional Returns: The RBI may introduce new returns / withdraw
existing returns (both ad-hoc/ regular) for submission by SEs and inform such entities
suitably.
requirements.object: withdraw existing returns (both ad-hoc/ regular) for submission by SEs and inform such entities suitably

requirements.full_sentence:
Mode of submission of Returns: SEs shall submit all the returns through online
mode in the formats and in the manner as communicated to them (See Annex III),
unless specified otherwise.
requirements.object: through online mode in the formats and in the manner as communicated to them (See Annex III)

requirements.full_sentence:
Returns submitted in hard copy format through hand
delivery/ post/ courier, or in soft copy format through e-mails, shall not be accepted
(i.e., would not be deemed to have been submitted by SEs), unless prescribed.
requirements.object: not be deemed to have been submitted by SEs)

conditions: in case of non-availability of on-line portals
requirements.full_sentence:
As a
contingency measure, in case of non-availability of on-line portals, SEs may be
advised to submit the returns through email.
requirements.object: the returns through email

requirements.full_sentence:
However, SEs shall re-submit the return
through online mode soon after its availability.
requirements.object: online mode soon after its availability

requirements.full_sentence:
Penalties: All the SEs shall furnish the correct and true information in the returns
prescribed in the Directions within the stipulated timelines.
requirements.object: true information in the returns prescribed in the Directions within the stipulated timelines

conditions:
In case any SE is found in
violation of these Directions
requirements.full_sentence:
In case any SE is found in
violation of these Directions, the Reserve Bank may take necessary action including
imposition of a penalty / fine under the extant provisions of the Banking Regulation
Act, 1949 or the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934 or the Securitization and
Reconstruction of Financial Assets and Enforcement of Security Interest Act, 2002,
as the case may be.
requirements.object: imposition of a penalty / fine under the extant provisions of the Banking Regulation Act, 1949 or the Reserve Bank of

requirements.full_sentence:
Notwithstanding the abovementioned repeals, anything done, or any action taken
or purported to have been done or taken, or any direction given, or any proceeding
taken or any penalty or fine imposed under the repealed enactments shall, insofar as
it is not inconsistent with the provisions of these Directions, be deemed to have been
done or taken under the corresponding provisions of these Directions.
requirements.object: is not inconsistent with the provisions of these Directions, be deemed to have been done or taken under the corresponding provisions o

requirements.full_sentence: Returns for the quarter ending months i.e., June, September, December and March may be submitted within in 21 days.
requirements.object: 21 days

requirements.full_sentence: Returns for all the months shall be submitted within 15 days.
requirements.object: days

requirements.full_sentence:
Local Area Banks (LABs) - Designating officials responsible for correct and timely submission of data
'Two senior officials may be designated who would be responsible for the correct compilation and timely submission of the returns and who
LABs shall nominate a senior officer (of rank of General Manager or equivalent) to coordinate the compilation of the data and to be
Return Name Existing guidelines Revised guidelines would be fully responsible for the information furnished therein.'
responsible to the Reserve Bank for submission of the returns in time.
requirements.object: (of rank of General Manager or equivalent) to coordinate the compilation of the data and to be Return Name Existing guidelin

requirements.full_sentence:
In section 2, all
borrower groups having total exposure in excess of 30 % of bank's capital funds
or a minimum of fifty large borrower groups should be reported irrespective of the
percentage of such exposure to bank's capital funds;
requirements.object: the percentage of such exposure to bank's capital funds

requirements.full_sentence:
In case, any entity to whom the
return is applicable does not have any borrower with aggregate exposure of ₹5
crores and above for a reporting month, it shall submit a 'NIL' return.

In case, any entity to whom the return is applicable
does not have any large borrower with default move-in/ move-out positions during
the week, it shall submit a 'NIL' return.

For reporting in CPR, the consolidation may exclude group
companies which are engaged in (a) insurance business and (b) Businesses not
pertaining to financial services.
requirements.object: are engaged in (a) insurance business and (b) Businesses not pertaining to financial services

requirements.full_sentence:
Allocation of branches to Statutory Branch Auditors (SBAs) by Public Sector
Banks (PSBs): Public Sector Banks are required to upload the details of branches
allocated to their SBAs, on an annual basis, in the Auditor Allocation System (AAS)
application maintained by the Bank.
requirements.object: of branches allocated to their SBAs, on an annual basis, in the Auditor Allocation System (AAS) application maintained by the Ba

requirements.full_sentence:
Half yearly/ Quarterly Review of Accounts of Public Sector Banks - Review
Report by Statutory Central Auditors (SCAs): Statutory Central Auditors of
PSBs are required to conduct half yearly/quarterly review of their accounts.
requirements.object: review of their accounts

requirements.full_sentence:
Half yearly/ Quarterly Review of Accounts of Public Sector Banks - Review
of Accounts: Public Sector Banks are required to submit the information on half
yearly/quarterly review of their accounts as per the prescribed format.
requirements.object: on half yearly/quarterly review of their accounts as per the prescribed format

requirements.full_sentence:
In case, any
entity to whom the return is applicable does not have any borrower with aggregate
exposure of ₹5 crores and above for a reporting month, it shall submit a 'NIL'
return.

Applicable NBFCs shall submit the
following two ALM returns:
requirements.object: ALM returns:

requirements.full_sentence:
In case, any entity to whom the return is applicable
does not have any borrower with aggregate exposure of ₹5 crores and above for
a reporting month, it shall submit a 'NIL' return.

DNBS09 - CRILC Weekly Return on Defaulted Borrowers (RDB)- NBFCs may
report certain information on large borrowers (having aggregate exposure of ₹5
crores and above with them), who have defaulted during a week or moved out of
the default position, through this return on each Friday.
requirements.object: large borrowers (having aggregate exposure of ₹5 crores and above with them), who have defaulted during a week or moved

requirements.full_sentence:
In case, any entity to whom
the return is applicable does not have any large borrower with default move-in/
move-out positions during the week, it shall submit a 'NIL' return.

DNBS10 -Statutory Auditor's Certificate (SAC) - Yearly Return: All NBFCs and
ARCs shall enable their Statutory Auditors to submit 'DNBS10 - Statutory Auditor's
Certificate (SAC) Return' every year.
requirements.object: to submit 'DNBS10 - Statutory Auditor's Certificate (SAC) Return' every year

requirements.full_sentence:
The certificate shall be based on audited
books of accounts of the applicable entity, for the preceding financial year.
requirements.object: books of accounts of the applicable entity, for the preceding financial year

requirements.full_sentence:
The Statutory Auditor shall compile, generate and file the return, using the secure
login credentials created by the applicable entity.
requirements.object: the return, using the secure login credentials created by the applicable entity

requirements.full_sentence:
DNBS13 - Overseas Investment Return: All the NBFCs (irrespective of category,
business classification and asset size) shall submit information on their overseas
investment and returns on a quarterly basis.
requirements.object: overseas investment and returns on a quarterly basis

requirements.full_sentence:
In case, there are no overseas
investments during the reporting quarter, a 'NIL' return shall be submitted.

Form A Certificate - All NBFCs
These returns may continue be submitted in hard copy, or, through e-mails as
prescribed, till provisions are made for submission of these returns through online
portals.
requirements.object: hard copy, or, through e-mails as prescribed, till provisions are made for submission of these returns through online portals
